name: Waffles
recipeIngredient: 2 cups liquid (water, juice, soy milk, etc.)
recipeInstructions:
1. Measure the dry ingredients: oatmeal, cornmeal, whole wheat flour, baking powder, and pumpkin pie spice.
2. Prepare the egg substitute according to package directions.
3. In a large bowl, add the Prune Puree (see recipe below) and mix in 1 tbsp of it for every 1 tbsp of fat called for in the recipe.
4. Gradually add the liquid ingredients (water, juice, soy milk, etc.) to the dry ingredients while stirring until just combined.
5. Heat a waffle iron according to manufacturer's instructions.
6. Pour the batter onto the hot waffle iron and cook until golden brown and crispy.
7. Serve warm with maple syrup, fruit, or fruit syrup, as desired.
Prune Puree:
1. Combine 1-1/3 cups (8 oz.) pitted prunes and 6 tbsp water in a food processor container.
2. Pulse the mixture on and off until the prunes are finely chopped.
3. This puree can be used as a substitute for fat in baking recipes, using 1 tbsp of puree for each 1 tbsp of fat called for.
4. Store any leftover puree in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to one week.
